name: OmniLite: Accessible Multiomics Analysis Platform
description: An open-source, lightweight multiomics analysis platform designed for researchers in resource-limited settings. Enables complex genomic analyses (scRNA-seq + ATAC-seq integration) for infectious disease research without requiring high-end computational infrastructure.

breakdown.description: Full-time development work by MS student for 12 months
GPU credits for AI model training, cloud testing, and scalability validation
Public dataset acquisition and preprocessing for TB, dengue, and AMR research
Workshops for 3-5 Indian labs, tutorial videos, and documentation
Open-access publication fees, conference travel, and long-term maintenance

needs: Cloud computing resources for testing and validation

impact.target_users: Researchers in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) working on infectious diseases
impact.problem: Multiomics analysis requires expensive computational infrastructure and proprietary tools, creating barriers for researchers in resource-limited settings
impact.solution: Lightweight, open-source platform that runs on low-spec machines with pre-built workflows for TB, dengue, and antimicrobial resistance research

milestones.name: Validation & Testing
deliverables: Validation on Indian infectious disease datasets

milestones.name: Community Deployment
deliverables: Public release v1.0 with user support

long_term_plan: Build active user community in LMIC research institutions
Establish partnerships with bioinformatics training programs
Apply for follow-on grants (CZI EOSS, Wellcome Trust)
sustainability.open_source_commitment: All code will remain open-source under MIT license with transparent development practices

members.name: Himanshu Bhandary
members.affiliation: MS Data Science, Chandigarh University, India

team.advisors: Seeking faculty collaboration at Chandigarh University for scientific guidance
